title: Nucor Corporation (NUE) Is a Trending Stock: Facts to Know Before Betting on It
text: Nucor (NUE) has received quite a bit of attention from Zacks.com users lately. Therefore, it is wise to be aware of the facts that can impact the stock's prospects.

title: Nucor (NUE) Expects Steel Mills Growth to Boost Q2 Earnings
text: Nucor (NUE) expects improved second-quarter earnings sequentially, driven by strong performance in steel mills and raw materials segments.

title: Nucor (NUE) Sets Utility Structures Production Plant's Location
text: The new utility infrastructure production plant by Nucor (NUE) is expected to produce 200 full-time jobs.

title: Nucor plans to hire 200 people for new utility infrastructure plant in Indiana
text: Nucor Corp. NUE said Wednesday it plans to create 200 full-time jobs for its second utility structures production plant in Crawfordsville, Ind., as part of the growth of its Towers & Structures unit.

title: Investors Heavily Search Nucor Corporation (NUE): Here is What You Need to Know
text: Recently, Zacks.com users have been paying close attention to Nucor (NUE). This makes it worthwhile to examine what the stock has in store.

title: Nucor Is Going Nuclear: What Investors Should Know
text: At one point in its life, Nucor sold products to the nuclear power industry. Today, it is one of the largest steelmakers in North America.

title: ExxonMobil Signs Deal to Capture Carbon From Nucor's Plant
text: ExxonMobil (XOM) will capture up to 800,000 metric tons of CO2 per annum from Nucor's (NUE) facility and store the same at its Louisiana facility.

title: Commodity Stocks Are Cheap. Time to Buy Soon.
text: The Energy Select Sector SPDR ETF is down about 16% from a multiyear peak in late 2022, and the SPDR S&P Metals & Mining ETF is down about 30%.

title: ExxonMobil, Nucor Ink Big Carbon Capture Deal
text: ExxonMobil and big steel-maker Nucor announced a major carbon capture and storage (CCS) deal Thursday.

title: Exxon signs decarbonization contract with steel maker Nucor
text: Exxon Mobil Corp. said on Wednesday it signed with U.S. steel maker Nucor Corporation a carbon capture and sequestration (CCS) contract, its first from a hard to abate industry.

title: This Is Why U.S. Steel Wants to Be More Like Nucor
text: U.S. Steel is an icon in the steel industry, though it no longer dominates the industry like it once did. A big drawback of its business model is its reliance on blast furnaces.
